content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of task-gated, retrieval-augmented active memory for agents can resolve a longstanding flaw in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND): the propensity to get stuck in uninformative noisy or infinite curiosity loops (e.g. spamming random noise inputs to maximize prediction error). By dynamically prioritizing and pruning episodic curiosity-driven exploration experiences in an active learning memory framework, agents can automatically retain only high-value exploration traces that connect to downstream task goals, rather than rewarding any novel stimulus regardless of utility.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
